body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 20/2026
Made under: Interpretation Amendment Act
Published in: No. 4 – B.C. Regs. 18 to 28, March 10, 2026
Deposit date: March 2, 2026

B.C. Reg. 20/2026, deposited March 2, 2026, under theINTERPRETATION AMENDMENT ACT, 2019[section 6]. Order in Council 63/2026, approved and ordered March 2, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective March 9, 2026, (a)	theInterpretation Amendment Act, 2019, S.B.C. 2019, c. 41, is brought into force, and (b)	the Daylight Saving Time Regulation, B.C. Reg. 136/2006, is repealed. — N. SHARMA,Attorney General and Deputy Premier; C. BOYLE,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC repeals Daylight Saving Time Regulation, moves to permanent standard time
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has officially repealed the Daylight Saving Time Regulation and brought the Interpretation Amendment Act, 2019 into force. This means the province will no longer observe daylight saving time (the practice of moving clocks forward in spring and back in fall). Instead, BC will stay on standard time year-round, starting from the date the regulation was repealed (March 9, 2026). For business owners, this means no more clock changes twice a year, which can affect scheduling, contracts, and employee work hours.
